About Me

Finance Meets Data Science

I am a BBA Finance & FinTech student at the School of Business & Economics, United International University (UIU), Dhaka, Bangladesh — with expertise in financial analysis, Python-based data analytics, and investment risk modeling.

As Treasurer of UIU Finance Forum (promoted 3 times in 4 years), I have led recruitment, managed event budgets, and secured sponsorships for one of UIU's most active student organizations. I also served as Project Lead for Team Executive's Edge across all three FIN 4333 Financial Analytics projects.

My research on social media influencer marketing was a Finalist at UIU Undergraduate Research Symposium 2024 and is now published on ResearchGate. I am passionate about bridging traditional finance with modern technology to drive data-driven investment decisions.

Project Portfolio

FIN 4333 — Three Project Cycles

Each project built on the previous — from raw data to forecasting to professional publication.

Project 01 · Analytics-Ready Dataset
Grameenphone Ltd.
Financial Analytics Dataset · 2020–2024
Completed

Built a monthly analytics-ready dataset for Grameenphone Ltd. (GP), Bangladesh's largest telecom company, combining stock price data from Yahoo Finance, firm-level financial variables, and Bangladesh macroeconomic indicators via the World Bank API. Applied rigorous data alignment rules, quality checks, and documented a full data dictionary.

60 Monthly Rows12 Variables2020–2024World Bank APIAlignment Rule Applied
Data Structurefinal_monthly_dataset.csv
Timeframe
5 Years
Variables
12 Vars
Samples
60 Months
PythonpandasyfinanceWorld Bank APIopenpyxlJupyter
GARCH(1,1)
Volatility Model
Project 02 · Market Risk & Volatility Forecasting
NVIDIA (NVDA)
GARCH(1,1) Volatility Pipeline · 2020–2025
Completed

Estimated GARCH(1,1) volatility on NVIDIA daily log returns using ARIMA(2,0,3) as the mean equation. Produced a 30-day volatility outlook and prepared a professional investment risk memo. NVDA classified as HIGH RISK with annualized volatility of 41.56% and 30-day forecast of 49.19%.

ARIMA(2,0,3)GARCH(1,1)Ann. Vol: 41.56%30-Day: 49.19%HIGH RISK
Figure 1: NVDA GARCH(1,1) Conditional Volatility — Estimated vs 30-Day Forecast
Annualized Volatility
41.56%
30-Day Forecast
49.19%
Conclusion
NVDA carries elevated risk levels unsuitable for conservative portfolios.
PythonarchstatsmodelspmdarimaMatplotlibpython-docx
ARIMA vs LSTM
Return Forecasting
Project 03 · Return Forecasting — ARIMA vs LSTM
NVIDIA (NVDA)
ARIMA vs LSTM Return Forecasting · 2020–2025
Completed

Compared classical ARIMA benchmark against deep-learning LSTM for forecasting NVIDIA daily log returns. Used chronological 70/15/15 split with no data leakage. ARIMA(1,0,0) selected as recommended model — superior directional accuracy confirms LSTM complexity not justified for this efficient large-cap equity.

ARIMA(1,0,0) SelectedAIC: −4208.711,258 Observations30-Day: +0.0021
ModelTest MAETest RMSEDir. Accuracy
ARIMA(1,0,0) ✓0.0256250.03326653.44%
LSTM (50u, w=10)0.0251600.03264650.26%
Figure 2: NVDA Daily Log Return — Actual vs ARIMA(1,0,0) vs LSTM (Test Period)
Recommended Model
ARIMA(1,0,0)
Directional Accuracy
53.44%
Conclusion
LSTM\'s marginal RMSE edge does not justify its added complexity over ARIMA.

Transparency

AI Disclosure

Responsible AI Use
AI tools (Claude by Anthropic) were used to assist with Python code generation, document structuring, and analytical drafting across all three projects in this portfolio.
Verification & Validation
All AI outputs were independently reviewed, tested, and verified by team members. Model results (RMSE, MAE, AIC, directional accuracy) were cross-checked against raw notebook outputs.
Final Accountability
Final results, interpretations, and investment conclusions represent the team's own analysis and judgment. Submitted in full compliance with FIN 4333 responsible AI use guidelines.
